meta.name: CES-D-10 Depression Scale Short Form
meta.fullName: Center for Epidemiologic Studies Depression Scale-10
meta.description: The CES-D-10 is a 10-item short form of the CES-D used for rapid screening of depressive symptoms. Selected from the original 20-item version, it maintains good reliability and validity, making it particularly suitable for large-scale epidemiological surveys and primary care screening. It assesses the frequency of depression-related symptoms over the past week.
meta.author: Andresen, Malmgren, Carter & Patrick (1994)
meta.reference: Andresen EM, Malmgren JA, Carter WB, Patrick DL. Screening for depression in well older adults: evaluation of a short form of the CES-D. Am J Prev Med. 1994;10(2):77-84.
meta.instruction: Below is a list of some ways you may have felt or behaved. Please indicate how often you have felt this way during the past week.

commonChoices.label: Rarely or none of the time (less than 1 day)
Some or a little of the time (1-2 days)
Occasionally or a moderate amount of the time (3-4 days)

questions.text: I was bothered by things that usually don't bother me
I had trouble keeping my mind on what I was doing
I felt that everything I did was an effort
I felt hopeful about the future

ranges.label: No Depression
ranges.description: Your score is below the screening cutoff (10). No significant depressive symptoms are indicated. This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Keep up regular exercise and social activities
Monitor mood changes; occasional low mood is normal
Maintain regular social activities and hobbies
Set aside time each day for activities you enjoy

ranges.label: Mild Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score has reached the screening cutoff (>=10), indicating clinically significant depressive symptoms. These mild symptoms may occasionally affect your energy and motivation, but they typically do not interfere significantly with daily functioning.
suggestions: Practice self-care and maintain a regular sleep schedule
Increase physical exercise and social activities
Try talking to someone you trust about your feelings
Consider consulting a mental health professional for further evaluation
Try 30 minutes of aerobic exercise daily, such as walking or cycling

ranges.label: Moderate Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are at a moderate level and may be affecting your daily life and social functioning. At this level, feelings of low mood may cause persistent fatigue and reduced interest in activities, potentially affecting daily life and relationships.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist soon
Maintain a regular daily routine
Engage in physical exercise at least 3 times per week
Avoid alcohol and unhealthy coping strategies
Consider psychotherapy (e.g., cognitive behavioral therapy)

ranges.label: Severe Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are quite severe and are likely significantly affecting daily functioning. Please seek professional help promptly. Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Please seek medical attention from a psychiatrist as soon as possible
Medication combined with psychotherapy may be needed
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Do not stay alone; inform family or friends of your situation
Ensure you have a trusted support person nearby
